texts.full_text: home values could also open up refinancing possibilities at more favorable interest rates since lenders typically use current property value as collateral when issuing loans. If you've been considering refinancing but were previously unable to due high assessments, now might be an opportune time to explore your options again. Reduced Holding Costs for Investors For those who own investment properties on Long Island or elsewhere in New York State, declining values could mean lower holding costs associated with property taxes and maintenance expenses if they're able negotiate reduced rents due the softer market conditions . This can help maintain cash flow while waiting out what appears to be a temporary downturn cycle. The Downside? Of course, there are downsides as well: If you were planning on selling your home anytime soon then declining values could mean accepting lower offers than anticipated - unless of course you're also buying another property in which case decreased prices might work in your favor . Additionally , if this trend continues long-term it may negatively impact local economies reliant upon real estate activity . What’s Next? It's important note that while recent data suggests a weakening Long Island housing market, no one can predict with certainty how long this trend will last or whether it represents merely a temporary correction within an otherwise robust economy. Homeowners should carefully weigh the potential benefits against risks before making any major financial decisions based on current conditions .

texts.full_text: F), expresses how much stronger a system is than it needs to be for an intended load. Safety factors are often calculated using detailed analysis because comprehensive testing is impractical on many projects, such as bridges and buildings, but the structure's ability to carry a load must be determined to a reasonable accuracy. Many systems are intentionally built much stronger than needed for normal usage to allow for emergency situations, unexpected loads, misuse, or degradation (reliability). Definition There are two definitions for the factor of safety (FoS): The ratio of a structure's absolute strength (structural capability) to its operating load. Mathematically, it can be expressed as: \[ \text{Factor of Safety} = \frac{\text{Absolute Strength}}{\text{Operating Load}} \] 2. The inverse ratio of the applied stress or strain in a system to the ultimate allowable stress (or allowable strain). This is given by: \[ \text{Factor of Safety} = \frac{\text{Ultimate Allowable Stress}}{\text{Applied Stress}} \] Both definitions aim to ensure that structures and systems are robust enough to handle unexpected conditions beyond their intended use. The factor of safety plays a crucial role in ensuring the reliability, durability, and ultimately the s

texts.full_text: voice. This was our first foray into a consumer-generated campaign. Q: So how did the campaign (with agency MindComet) work? A: We really wanted to promote the fact that many of our competi­tors don't have flame grills in their restaurants. We wanted to point out that difference and drive it home for our target audience. At first, we had these really high hopes of getting consumers to take video cameras into our competitors and ask thecashier where the flame grill was and see what kind of responses we had. But when we met with our counsel, they said that we couldn't do that. We learned so much from the legal end on this. Q: How did you reach consumers and what did they have to do? A: We promoted it with an e-mail campaign and through blog posts on our site ElPolloLoco.com. We asked consumers to create a small video describing why El Pollo Loco flame grilled chicken is so much better than any other way of cook­ing chicken. Q: What was the response rate? A: We had more than 117 videos sent to us in 30 days. We had 30,000 new views on the microsite that had a duration of more than 2.5 minutes. So we had a lot of good interaction with consumers. All the books listed will work with the smaller Kindle, but the DX's bigger screen makes it ideal for these optimized titles. Here are some quick tips from my book: 1. KindleGen: Use Amazon’s free tool called "KindleGen" to convert your PDFs and other formats into MOBI files that work perfectly on your Kindle, especially the DX model with its larger screen real estate. 2. texts.full_text: m a radiation source. Because the integral is taken only over the slit, the slit appears to act like an effective source of radiation, in accordance with the Huygens principle [@Schuocker1998]. This principle states that wave fronts passing through slits act as effective sources, reradiating spherical waves. It is not intuitively obvious that the particles passing through the slits should be deflected to recreate spherical wavE patterns. Paradox is that how does the particle identify positions of all slits? @ZeilengerEtAl2003 have written “The wave-particle duality states that the description of one and the same physical object suggests the local particle picture in the source and on the screen, but a wave model for the unobserved propagation of the object.” While the particles go through the slits one by one, a single particle always gives a single click at a detector. texts.full_text: voice on the Times editorial page. Before joining The New York Times, he was a senior editor at The Atlantic. His published books are Bad Religion: How We Became a Nation of Heretics (2012), Grand New Party (2008) with Reihan Salam, and Privilege: Harvard and the Education of the Ruling Class (2005). He frequently appeared on the video debate site Bloggingheads.tv until 2012. David Brooks called Grand New Party the "best single roadmap of where the Republican Party should and is likely to head." Douthat was a film critic for National Review and has also contributed to The Wall Street Journal, The Weekly Standard, the Claremont Review of Books, GQ, Slate, and other publications. Published works Grand New Party: How Republicans Can Win the Working Class and Save the American Dream. With Salam, Reihan. New York: Doubleday. 2008. . The Decadent Society: How We Became the Victims of Our Own Success, Avid Reader Press / Simon & Schuster, 2020. texts.full_text: was a tad suspect. We recovered our $20,000 and rewelded the equipment with forgings that welded beautifully. All is well that ends well (For us anyways!) I enjoyed reading the comments. Jan 18, 2008 Rating Segregation by: Timothy E. Volin, Ph.D. Interesting! I suspect that the problem is a result of the use of a casting rather than a forging or other wrought product. Stainless 317LMN is a complex alloy with a lot of alloying additions. As a consequence of this there can be a significant range of solidification temperature, that is, a range of temperature over which both solid phase and liquid phase co-exist. Segregation of the alloying additions occurs between the liquid phase and solid phase, leading to differences in composition between the initial solid phase formed, and the final solidification of the remaining liquid phase. The alloying additions that tend to depress the solidification temperature of the remaining liquid phase, particularly Silicon, Phosphorous, Sulfur, are particularly strongly segregated to the remaining liquid phase. The result is that there will be regions in the solidified structure, the last areas to solidify, that have a melting point well below what

This is an exciting opportunity for a dynamic VoluThe Landmarks What We Do Programs Program Delivery About Us Meet the Team Our Partners Careers Contact Us Volunteer Donate Volunteer Coordinator The Irvine Ranch Conservancy is a mission-based non-profit organization with 50 full-time, part-time, and seasonal employees and over 400 volunteers. Our mission is to protect, restore, and enhance the natural resources of nearly 50,000 acres of protected open space on the Irvine Ranch Natural Landmarks in perpetuity, and to provide diverse opportunities for compatible public enjoyment through scientific, recreational, and educational initiatives and programs. This is an exciting opportunity for a dynamic Volunteer Coordinator to work with a team of staff and volunteers to coordinate and support volunteer programs. The Volunteer Coordinator is responsible for all aspects of volunteer coordination and administration, including volunteer recruitment, orientation, training, retention, and recognition. The Volunteer Coordinator is also responsible for all aspects of volunteer program administration, including volunteer data entry, database management, and reporting. The Volunteer Coordinator reports to the Director of Volunteer Programs and works closely with the Volunteer Manager and other staff to support volunteer programs. texts.full_text: 77 Humanity is defined as: human race: the human race considered as a whole 2. qualities of human being: the qualities or characteristics considered as a whole to be characteristic of human beings 3. kindness: kindness or compassion for others This would seem to indicate that in order for war or aggression to take place that an intrinsic part of one’s humanity would have to be abandoned. How can one human inject a bullet into the head of another and still feel kindness or compassion towards their fellow man? The answer may lie in the dehumanization of the enemy, as well as the desensitization that often occurs through exposure to violence. When individuals begin to view others as less than human, it becomes easier to justify aggressive actions against them. Additionally, societal norms and values can play a role in shaping one's understanding of humanity and determining when such acts are deemed acceptable. Ultimately, balancing the concepts of aggression and compassion is an ongoing challenge for humankind, highlighting the need for continued introspection and dialogue

texts.full_text: Eurecat, presented during Smart City Expo World Congress in Barcelona the results of a project set to analyse tourists’ behaviour and flow using LBASense sensors, at the world renowned Sagrada Familia. The pilot project and the analysis of the data retrieved through it was led by the Ajuntament de Barcelona (Municipality of Barcelona) and developed together with Eurecat and Mobile World Capital Foundation in Barcelona, with the support of GSMA. The initiative aThe initiative aimed to enhance the management of the influx of tourists to the iconic Sagrada Familia, a symbol of Barcelona. The Sagrada Familia, a World Heritage Site and a masterpiece of the famed architect, Antoni Gaudí, attracts tourists and visitors from all over the globe. The influx, while a boon to the local economy, posed logistical and sustainability challenges, necessitating a solution to manage the tourists' experience and the city's tourism sustainability efforts. The implementation of the Internet of Things (IoT) and the utilization of the LBASense sensors, a local Barcelona solution, facilitated the real-time and precise capture of tourists' movements and density, addressing the challenges of the city's tourism management. The LBASense sensors, a cutting-edge solution, allowed the city and the tourism management entities to capture precise real-time data, which, in the subsequent data processing and analysis, could allow a better management of the tourists' influx, and the city's tourism in a broader sense. texts.full_text: which suggested this region as harboring one or more breast cancer susceptibility genes. To further investigate the chromosome 17q11.2-12 locus, we performed mutation screening of candidate genes within and around this interval in a panel of familial non-BRCA1/BRCA2 tumors. This included detailed sequencing analysis to identify potential pathogenic mutations that could contribute to breast cancer predisposition. The region on chromosome 17q11.2-12 is known for harboring several important tumor suppressor genes and other genetic elements involved in cellular functions such as DNA repair, cell cycle control, and apoptosis. One of the most prominent candidates within this locus is NF1 (Neurofibromin 1), although it primarily associates with neurofibromatosis type 1 rather than breast cancer. Our mutation screening focused on genes that had biological plausibility in breast tumorigenesis or showed evidence from other studies suggesting a role. We also considered regulatory regions and non-coding RNAs within the interval, as alterations in these elements can affect gene expression patterns critical for maintaining normal cellular function. The results of this study will contribute to our understanding of genetic factors involved in familial breast cancer beyond BRCA1/BRCA2 mutations. Identifying novel predisposing genes could have significant implications for clinical management and risk assessmen

texts.full_text: ave time to develop meaningful relationships. There’s no middle ground, others complain, where’s the route between frat party flings and near-obsessive-already-planning-the-wedding-in-Mem-Chu couplings? And sure, there are more people who want to be in relationships than actually are. Depending on your metrics, that might be sufficient data to prove the there’s-no-dating-at-Stanford hypothesis. But I don’t think that dating at Stanford is fundamentally flawed. I think that many of us are just going about it the wrong way. We Stanford students like for things to be effortless. High school valedictorians, sports stars, musical prodigies – you name it, we’re used to things coming easily. We focus on our academics and extracurricular activities and often assume that the rest will fall into place without any additional work on our part. Cue LikeaLittle.com, the very definition of half-hearted, lazy pursuit of meaningful relationships. Oh, sure, it’s much easier to confess that crush under the guise of anonymity of the Internet. But that post took you, what, 30 seconds to write? With relationships, as with any other meaningful pursuits, you receive according to the effort you put into something. So if your admiration for someone is really only worth a 30-second post, go for it. And watch absolutely nothing happen. Don’t accuse flirting of failing you – pin the blame on the sad excuse for a flirt medium in which you engaged. In real life, there are no fairy godmothers to make your wishes come true. If you want something to work out, TALK to the object of your affections. Yeah, it’s difficult and potentially awkward, but we’re all too busy to assess the intricacies of chance meetings. If you’re actually interested in someone, you really “need to be bold, need to jump in the cold water” and put yourself out there. If you don’t put forth at least that much effort and interest, why should they? And for the hesitant out there: honestly, what’s the worst that could happen? The math is in your favor. If it

texts.full_text: e Penguins, now there's swim of the penguins. New research reveals Fiordland penguins travel up to 7000 kilometres in eight weeks to reach their wintering grounds off Australia’s east coast and Tasmania before returning home again each year for breeding season! This extraordinary journey is a testament not only these birds' endurance but also highlights the importance of protecting marine environments along migration routes. The study provides valuable insights into conservation efforts aimed at safeguarding this species during its annual trek across vast oceanic distance
